resources:
PlayTranslate
Translate Use Region Once Change
Capture
Region Translate %1$s Settings
From To
PlayTranslate uses the Accessibility Service to take screenshots of the game screen and show a floating overlay icon. No UI content, input events, or personal data are read or monitored. Accessibility permission needed PlayTranslate needs the Accessibility permission to capture screenshots of the game screen on the top display while staying open on the bottom display.

PlayTranslate does not read any UI content, monitor input, or access any personal data — it only takes screenshots when you tap Translate or start live mode.

Go to Settings → Accessibility → Installed apps → PlayTranslate → Enable. Open Accessibility Settings Enable PlayTranslate in Accessibility Settings first
Tap Translate to capture the game screen Hold any bottom button to quickly switch capture region No words were detected Capturing screen… Recognising text… Translating… Downloading translation model… No %1$s text detected in "%2$s" Error: %1$s
playtranslate_capture Screen Capture PlayTranslate is active Tap to return to PlayTranslate
Allow Notifications Android requires apps that run in the background to display a status bar notification while active.

This will never send alerts, sounds, or banners — only a quiet persistent indicator that PlayTranslate is running. Enable Accessibility PlayTranslate uses Accessibility to:
• Capture the game screen
• Show a floating overlay icon on the game screen
• Show a region preview on the game screen
• Add custom capture regions

It does not read UI content, monitor input, or access personal data. Screen recording works, but region preview and custom regions won't be available. Grant Permission Open Accessibility Settings Use screen recording instead Capture Method Accessibility enabled — full functionality Screen recording active Enable Accessibility to use region preview on the game screen and create custom capture regions. Enable Accessibility for PlayTranslate To see a preview of your selected region on the game screen, you must enable Accessibility Settings. This is also necessary to create new capture regions. Tap here to change. Accessibility required Adding custom regions requires the Accessibility permission. It's used to draw a live preview box on the game screen so you can see exactly what area you're selecting.

Go to Settings → Accessibility → Installed apps → PlayTranslate → Enable. Allow notifications Android requires apps that run in the background to show a status bar notification. PlayTranslate uses this to indicate it is active while translating.

This will never send alerts, sounds, or banners — only a quiet, persistent status indicator.
Reading Part of speech Meanings JLPT Tap any word in the original text to look it up No dictionary entry found for "%1$s" Dictionary lookup failed.
Japanese → English
Settings Translation Language Only Japanese → English is currently supported. More languages coming soon. Capture display Select the display that shows the game. Usually Display 0 is the main/top screen. No displays found DeepL API Key (optional) Without a key, PlayTranslate translates via Google's unofficial translate endpoint (no account needed), with on-device ML Kit as an offline fallback. Add a DeepL key to use DeepL as the primary translator. Unfortunately, the free plan still requires a credit card, but translations are better and it includes 500,000 characters per month: https://www.deepl.com/en/pro#developer Paste your DeepL API key here ⚠ No internet connection — showing offline translation (lower quality). ⚠ Translation service unavailable — showing offline translation (lower quality).
Original Translation Words Looking up words…
Region Select Region Edit Done + Add custom region Add Custom Region Drag the region box on the game screen to select your desired area. Drag the top or bottom edge, or drag the middle to move the whole box. Region name Rename region e.g. Dialogue box
Show on-screen icon Drag this icon over words for quick definition lookups, or tap it for more features. This is required to access PlayTranslate features while playing your game. Accessibility required The floating overlay icon requires the Accessibility permission to draw on the game screen.

Go to Settings → Accessibility → Installed apps → PlayTranslate → Enable.
Enable Accessibility PlayTranslate needs the Accessibility permission to work on single-screen devices.

With it, PlayTranslate can:
• Show a floating overlay icon on your screen
• Show translated text as an overlay over your game
• Capture the game screen without leaving the app
• Display custom capture regions on your screen

It does not read UI content, monitor input, or access personal data. \ Show Translation Hide translation Skip automatic translation. Tap "Show Translation" on the results screen to see it on demand. Show transliteration Display romanized reading below the original text.
Anki Deck Install AnkiDroid to create flashcards AnkiDroid is a free spaced-repetition flashcard app. Connect it to PlayTranslate to save translated sentences and vocabulary — with the original text, translation, and a screenshot — directly to your Anki decks. https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.ichi2.anki Get AnkiDroid on Google Play → AnkiDroid permission needed To add flashcards to Anki, PlayTranslate needs permission to access AnkiDroid.

Tap Continue to grant this permission. Continue To add flashcards to Anki, PlayTranslate needs permission to access AnkiDroid. Permission denied — grant it in AnkiDroid settings Loading decks… No decks found in AnkiDroid Added to Anki! Failed to add card to Anki Add to Anki Grant AnkiDroid Access Remove Screenshot Sentence Word Bold target word in sentence Bold "%1$s" in sentence
